title: nuphar lutea subsp. ozarkana
intro: Nuphar lutea subsp. ozarkana, also known as Ozark water lily or American water lily, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the Nuphar genus. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Nuphar lutea subsp. ozarkana include treating fever, skin conditions, and digestive issues, as well as being used as a diuretic and to reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by extracting the roots and rhizomes of the plant.
